tldr: Different layers: Devenv is a dev environment provisioner, Please Build is a hermetic build system — usually you adopt Please alongside or instead of make/scripts, while keeping or replacing Devenv as a separate decision.
why_switch: Need hermetic, incremental, polyglot builds across Go/Python/Java/JS
Want remote build caching shared with CI
Bazel-style fine-grained targets with lighter setup than Bazel
Single Go binary — avoid Nix install if Devenv was only for env
Better build performance for large monorepos than ad-hoc scripts
what_to_export: Existing build commands (make targets, npm scripts, shell scripts)
Source layout per language (Go modules, Python packages, Java projects)
Artifact outputs and where they're produced
CI build pipeline commands that ran inside devenv shell
Toolchain versions you need at build time
export_method: No automated converter. Document current build graph: inputs, outputs, and commands per package. Devenv's devenv.nix typically does not contain build rules (it provides the environment), so most extraction comes from Make/scripts. Keep this inventory as the source for writing BUILD files. Confirm rule names and syntax against the official Please documentation.
import_method: Install Please via the official one-line installer described in Please's docs. Run the init command at the repo root, which scaffolds a .plzconfig and example BUILD files. Author BUILD files per package using the appropriate language rules (e.g. go_library, python_library — verify current names). Wire remote caching per official guide. Keep Devenv (or replacement) for tool installs separately, since Please assumes tools are on PATH. Verify all rule names and config keys against current Please docs.

concept_mapping.note: Each package gets a BUILD file declaring rules
Please can manage some toolchains; consult docs for current options
Per-language test rules; verify exact names
Configure per official remote cache docs

gotchas: Authoring BUILD files for an existing repo is the bulk of the work
Please and Devenv are not substitutes — you may keep both initially
Smaller rule ecosystem than Bazel; some custom rules may be needed
Hermetic builds require pinning toolchain locations — non-trivial
Remote cache setup adds operational overhead
Verify all rule names and config syntax against current Please documentation before writing

email.subject: Proposal: introduce Please Build for hermetic monorepo builds
email.body:
Hi team,

Devenv covers our dev environments well, but our build commands are a tangle of Make and shell. I'd like to pilot Please Build for hermetic, cached, polyglot builds with fine-grained targets.

This is additive, not a Devenv replacement — different layer. Pilot scope: one service, write BUILD files, wire remote cache, measure CI savings. Estimated 1-3 weeks for the pilot.

Worth a try?

Thanks
